title: Watch NASA's new Mars helicopter rotor break the speed of sound (video)
summary: NASA is testing a new Mars helicopter rotor that breaks the speed of sound, aiming to develop next-generation aircraft for flying in Mars' thin atmosphere. The breakthrough could enable advanced exploration missions on the Red Planet.

title: Venus and a slender crescent moon steal the show after sunset on May 18
summary: Venus and a slender crescent moon were visible together in the evening sky on May 18. This celestial event was best observed just after sunset.

title: SpaceX Dragon capsule delivers science and supplies to space station
summary: A SpaceX Dragon cargo spacecraft delivered 6,500 pounds of science and supplies to the International Space Station on May 17, 2026. This marks another successful resupply mission for SpaceX.

title: Watch an asteroid the size of a blue whale hurtle towards Earth live online May 18
summary: A livestream on May 18 will show an asteroid the size of a blue whale approaching Earth in near real-time from robotic telescopes in Italy. The event is scheduled to begin at 3:45 p.m. EDT, weather permitting.

title: Giant radio telescope sees Artemis 2 astronauts on Orion flying around the moon. 'There are 4 people in those pixels.'
summary: A giant radio telescope in West Virginia tracked Artemis 2's Orion spacecraft around the moon for five days, gathering precise observations on its movements during the historic mission. The telescope captured the four astronauts aboard the Orion in its observations.

title: Get $341 off the limited X edition of the Vaonis Vespera II at Walmart
summary: Walmart is offering a $341 discount on the limited X edition of the Vaonis Vespera II telescope. The Vespera II X edition includes a transparent shell and internal temperature sensors for improved deep sky observation.

title: With the Vespera III and Vespera Pro 2, telescope-maker Vaonis unveils its sharpest optics yet
summary: Vaonis has launched its new Vespera III and Vespera Pro 2 telescopes, featuring sharper optics and upgraded features for improved stargazing. The new models are now available for purchase.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 210 — ESCAPADES at Mars
summary: Episode 210 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast discusses the Mars ESCAPADE mission, featuring a conversation with Dr. Robert Lillis. The episode focuses on the details and objectives of the ESCAPADE mission to Mars.

title: NASA's Psyche spacecraft is flying to a metal asteroid. So, why did it just visit Mars?
summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft performed a flyby of Mars to utilize a gravity assist, which helps it gain speed and conserve fuel for its journey to a metal asteroid. This maneuver is a common technique in space exploration to optimize spacecraft trajectories.

title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch on 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission to Station
summary: NASA's 34th SpaceX resupply mission launched to the International Space Station with new scientific experiments aboard a Falcon 9 rocket. The mission lifted off from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station carrying nearly 6,500 pounds of cargo.

title: Curiosity Shakes Loose a Pesky Rock
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover successfully removed a stuck rock from its drill after days of troubleshooting. The rock had become lodged during a drilling operation on April 25, 2026.

title: Hubble Sights Galaxy in Transition
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy with a bright center and hints of a spiral structure, though it lacks distinct spiral arms. The galaxy features reddish-brown dust filaments and clumps, with background galaxies visible through its outer regions.

title: Picturing Earth in a New Light
summary: A recent analysis highlighted changes in artificial light at night across Earth, showing areas where it has increased and decreased. This study provides new insights into global light pollution patterns.

title: NASA Draws on Industry for Mars Telecommunications Network
summary: NASA has issued a Request for Proposal (RFP) to collaborate with industry on the Mars Telecommunications Network, aiming to establish reliable high-bandwidth communications for future Mars missions. The network will support surface, orbital, and human exploration by using high-performance Mars telecommunications orbiters.

title: NASA, Industry Prepare Cryogenic Fuel Technology Demo
summary: NASA is partnering with Eta Space to test cryogenic fuel technology in orbit through the Liquid Oxygen Flight Demonstration (LOXSAT), aiming to enable in-space propellant depots for future deep space missions.

title: Fresh Food Delivery for Space Station
summary: NASA astronauts and ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot enjoyed fresh food delivered by Northrop Grumman's Cygnus XL cargo spacecraft, which included oranges, apples, and onions, in April 2026.

title: Ice Moves Out of Aniak
summary: Ice melt along Alaska's Kuskokwim River has led to ice jams and subsequent flooding. This natural event highlights the impact of seasonal changes on local communities.

title: NASA-Supported Space Tech Advances Earthly Construction
summary: NASA-supported 3D printing technology developed for lunar habitats is now being applied to Earthly construction by Branch Technology Inc. The company's Freeform 3D Printing creates lightweight, efficient structures with lattice designs.

title: Rise Goes to Washington
summary: NASA's Artemis II zero gravity indicator, 'Rise,' was displayed during a congressional meeting in Washington with astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA's Jeremy Hansen. The mission, part of the Artemis program, aims to advance lunar exploration.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is actively planning and processing Artemis III, a significant crewed mission to the Moon. The mission includes key tests for the Human Landing System (HLS).

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. This follows multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. This marks a significant expansion for the company following its strong 2025 performance.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the CRS-34 mission to the International Space Station (ISS) on its third attempt. The launch occurred just two weeks after the Progress MS-34 spacecraft docked with the station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and Chinese missions are set to execute nine launches in the week of May 11. The launches span multiple locations in the United States and China.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 completed the Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19's successful static fire test. The milestone marks progress toward an upcoming launch.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing its deep space missions. The country's commercial sector is also making progress toward reusability in spaceflight.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion to support Flight 12, which is nearing readiness six months after Flight 11 concluded. The upgrade work is part of ongoing preparations for future missions.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two SpaceX Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission, potentially marking a calm period before a busier phase in spaceflight activity.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This publication commemorates five decades of significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency test and validate materials for spacecraft to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section supports these efforts with innovative ideas from emerging talent.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement. This initiative aims to enhance space exploration through immersive technologies.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical parachute testing process for a space mission, involving high-temperature exposure to simulate re-entry conditions. This bake-out procedure is essential for ensuring the parachute's reliability during atmospheric descent.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars landing by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 launch and search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could significantly improve satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has passed rigorous space-like tests, confirming its readiness for launch in early 2027 to search for Earth-like planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to provide feedback on space sector topics during the SME Forum 2026, held in Paris on June 8-9, 2026, with an online preparatory session in April-May.

title: Intuitive Machines Bets on Lunar Comms with Goonhilly Buy
summary: Intuitive Machines acquired Goonhilly Earth Station and its US subsidiary COMSAT to bolster its lunar communications capabilities. The move aims to provide integrated and resilient deep-space communication solutions for customers.

title: Iridium Boosts Air Safety Offering With Aireon Acquisition
summary: Iridium has acquired Aireon to enhance its air safety services by integrating space-based VHF communications. The acquisition aims to strengthen Iridium's offerings in aviation safety and global air traffic management.

title: European Space Startups Jockey for ‘Infrastructure’ Financing
summary: European space startups are seeking infrastructure financing as growth-stage capital remains scarce. The region faces challenges in securing late-stage funding for space ventures.

title: Guetlein: CBO is ‘Not Estimating What We’re Building’
summary: The head of the Golden Dome program criticized the Congressional Budget Office's $1.2 trillion cost estimate for missile-defense, stating it does not reflect the actual program being developed.

title: Meet Blacknight Space Labs In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator
summary: Blacknight Space Labs is launching an In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator offering up to $100,000 per company, along with mentorship and support for defense market entry and fundraising.

title: NASA Lays Out Preliminary Artemis III Mission Plan
summary: NASA has outlined the preliminary plan for the Artemis III mission, scheduled for 2027, which will test key capabilities in low Earth orbit before the planned Moon landing in 2028.

title: Exclusive: LEAP Launches its First Rocket
summary: LEAP has successfully launched its first rocket, marking its entry into the space industry. This achievement establishes LEAP as a new player in the launcher market.

title: CBO Puts $1.2T Price Tag on Golden Dome
summary: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) estimates the Golden Dome program will cost $1.2 trillion, significantly higher than the $185 billion figure proposed by its chief, Gen. Michael Guetlein, in March.

title: Exobiosphere, Voyager Partner on ISS Mission
summary: Exobiosphere has partnered with Voyager to deploy an Orbital High-Throughput Screening Device on the ISS. The automated mini lab is designed to fit into standard mid-deck lockers aboard crewed or uncrewed space stations.

title: NASA’s Launch Director Pick Turns Political
summary: Brian Hughes, NASA's first senior launch operations director, previously served as the agency's chief of staff but left after Administrator Jared Isaacman took over. His new role is seen as a political move.

title: What Will the Golden Dome Cost? Gen. Guetlein Pushes Back on CBO Cost Estimate
summary: The head of the Golden Dome program disputed a Congressional Budget Office (CBO) cost estimate for the system, which is based on a concept similar to a proposal from President Donald Trump. The disagreement highlights concerns over budget projections for the program.

title: IFC Market in China and India is On Cusp of Growth, Valour Predicts
summary: Valour Consultancy predicts significant growth for the In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) market in China and India. The consultancy's latest report highlights the potential scale and expansion of these markets.

title: Cowboy Space Files for 20,000-Satellite Constellation for Data Center Services
summary: Cowboy Space has filed for a 20,000-satellite constellation to provide data center services from space, following a $275 million Series B funding round. The company, previously rebranded, aims to leverage this massive satellite network for space-based data processing.

title: Iceye Delivers SAR Capability to Polish Armed Forces
summary: Iceye has delivered control over a radar satellite constellation to the Polish Armed Forces for sovereign intelligence within 12 months. This marks a significant step in enhancing Poland's space-based surveillance capabilities.

title: Kymeta Terminals to be Integrated Into Red Cat Uncrewed Surface Vessels
summary: Kymeta is partnering with Red Cat Futures to integrate its satcom terminals into uncrewed surface vessels. The collaboration was announced on May 14.

title: Major US Carries Band Together in Joint Venture to Encourage D2D Competition
summary: The three largest US mobile carriers—AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon—have formed a joint venture to promote direct-to-device (D2D) competition. This collaboration aims to simplify and enhance D2D service offerings for their customers.

title: Intuitive Machines Boosts Deep Space Communications With Goonhilly Acquisition
summary: Intuitive Machines has acquired Goonhilly Earth Station, a U.K.-based provider of deep space communication services. This acquisition aims to enhance Intuitive Machines' capabilities in deep space communications.

title: Iridium to Fully Acquire Aireon, Points to Space-Based VHF Opportunity
summary: Iridium is fully acquiring Aireon, a space-based air traffic surveillance system operator, expanding its stake from 39% to 100%. The move highlights opportunities in space-based VHF technology.

title: Space42 Reports Space Services Revenue Growth Despite Flat Results
summary: Space42 reported flat overall revenues for the three months ending March, but saw significant growth in space services revenue.

title: SpinLaunch to Collaborate with Aalyria for Network Orchestration
summary: SpinLaunch is collaborating with Aalyria Technologies to develop infrastructure for SpinLaunch’s Meridian LEO communications constellation. Aalyria will act as a strategic partner in this effort.
